articles.title: Trump confirms trade deal with India, tariffs slashed to 18%; Modi thanks him for ‘wonderful announcement’
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a reduced reciprocal tariff on India from 25% to 18% after holding a conversation with Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Monday. According to Trump's Truth Social post, India's trade deal with the US is "effective immediately". Trump detailed his conversation with PM Modi, saying that the latter had agreed to stop purchasing Russian oil and instead would purchase much more from the US and Venezuela. articles.title: Team Parenting: Why Anushka and Virat see raising kids as a shared journey
articles.summary: When Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli speak about parenting, what stands out is not dramatic emotion or grand declarations. It is the language of adjustment.Both have, in different interviews, acknowledged that becoming parents changed how they organise their time. Careers that once ran at full throttle now seem to move around family schedules. The change does not sound like sacrifice as much as recalibration. Something had to shift, and it seems to be the idea that work must always come first. Wh

articles.title: World News | President of Seychelles to Visit India This Month
articles.summary: New Delhi [India], February 2 (ANI): At the invitation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Patrick Herminie, the President of the Republic of Seychelles, will undertake a State Visit to India from February 5 to 10. This will be President Herminie's first visit to India since assuming office last October. articles.title: Why More Flyers Are Consciously Choosing Airbus Over Boeing
articles.summary: For many Indian flyers, July last year marked a turning point in how they feel about flying. When an Air India Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner, headed to London from Ahmedabad, crashed just 32 seconds after take-off, it didn’t just dominate headlines, it quietly settled into people’s minds. articles.title: India News | KTR Criticises Police Commissioner for Acting as 'Judge and Jury' in Alleged Phone-tapping Case
articles.summary: Hyderabad (Telangana) [India], February 2 (ANI): Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) Working President KT Rama Rao (KTR) on Monday criticised the police over what he called "high-handed actions" in the ongoing investigation into the alleged phone-tapping case. In a post on X, KTR said the Commissioner seemed to have acted as "judge and jury" in a case that is yet to be proven in court.
